paper_map.title: Mechanical properties of Fe–Al–M–C (M=Ti, V, Nb, Ta) alloys with strengthening carbides and Laves phase
paper_map.abstract_summary: The study investigates the mechanical properties of Fe-15Al and Fe-26Al based alloys with additions of 1 at.% C and 2 or 4 at.% Ti, V, Nb, or Ta. Room- and elevated-temperature compression and tension tests, along with hardness measurements and SEM fractography, are employed to assess yield stress, ductility, and fracture behavior. Results highlight strengthening by MC carbides and Laves phase, tension-compression asymmetry, and a yield stress anomaly in Fe-26Al alloys. The alloys containing Nb or Ta with C show promising properties for high-temperature applications.
paper_map.research_objective: To improve the room temperature ductility and high temperature strength of iron-aluminide alloys through precipitation strengthening by carbides and Laves phases.
main_process_variables: alloying element type (Ti, V, Nb, Ta)
testing temperature (room temperature to 800 °C, including yield stress anomaly range)
casting condition (vacuum induction melting, copper mould casting)
